raw_transcript.speaker: Robert
raw_transcript.text: Guten Morgen! Ich hätte gerne zwei Kipferln, bitte.

raw_transcript.speaker: Frau Berger
raw_transcript.text: Natürlich! Frisch gebacken. Sonst noch etwas?

raw_transcript.speaker: Robert
raw_transcript.text: Ja, ich nehme auch der Brot, bitte. Das dunkle.

raw_transcript.speaker: Frau Berger
raw_transcript.text: Sie meinen das Brot — sehr gerne! Das macht 3,40 Euro.

raw_transcript.speaker: Robert
raw_transcript.text: Hier ist fünf Euro. Äh... wie sagt man "to go" auf Deutsch?

raw_transcript.speaker: Frau Berger
raw_transcript.text: Man sagt "zum Mitnehmen". Einen Moment, ich pack das ein.

raw_transcript.speaker: Robert
raw_transcript.text: Danke schön. Das war sehr hilfreich!

raw_transcript.speaker: Frau Berger
raw_transcript.text: Bitte sehr! Und 1,60 Euro zurück. Einen schönen Tag noch!

reviewer_output.overall_summary: Robert handled a real-world bakery transaction confidently and naturally, using polite register throughout. The session contained only one clear grammatical error — a gender mistake on 'Brot' — and Robert showed good initiative by asking a vocabulary question in context, which is excellent learning behaviour.

errors.explanation: 'Brot' is neuter (das), so the accusative article is 'das', not 'der'.
errors.context: Ja, ich nehme auch der Brot, bitte.

vocabulary_highlights.note: used correctly — polite conditional, ideal for service interactions
used correctly — Austrian-specific term, shows regional awareness
new this session — learned by asking in context, excellent strategy
used correctly — natural and appropriate closing remark

vocabulary_highlights.english: Goodbye (lit. 'until we see each other again')
vocabulary_highlights.note: new this session — Austrian/Southern German alternative to 'Auf Wiedersehen'

strengths: Used the polite conditional 'Ich hätte gerne' correctly instead of the blunter 'Ich will'
Maintained formal Sie-register consistently throughout the interaction
Proactively asked a vocabulary question in German ('Wie sagt man...'), demonstrating good learner initiative
Handled numbers and the payment exchange naturally without hesitation
Used 'Ihnen auch' correctly as the formal response to a farewell wish
reviewer_output.next_focus: Practice noun genders for common food and everyday items (das Brot, das Brötchen, die Semmel, der Kaffee), using articles in both nominative and accusative cases to build automaticity.
